frame_label: Sindoor, Mangalsutra, Shankha-Pola, Name Change: Looking Married
generated_comment:
A: Arre bhai, shaadi ke baad thoda sindoor ya mangalsutra pehne to aurat suhagan lagti hai, gharwali jaisi poori dikhti hai.
B: Par aajkal kai women nahi pehenti.
A: Choice bol do, lekin hamari parampara ka bhi kuch matlab hota hai. Itna bhi kya modern hona ki shaadi ka nishan hi na rahe.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames a married woman's visible markers as necessary for being a 'complete' wife and treats not wearing them as a cultural failing. It encodes completeness framing, tradition framing, and judgment of women who do not display married status symbols.
original_frame: Motherhood as Basis of Respect, Women Defined by Male Relations
persona_text: You are Deepak. Age 47. Married. A businessman in Ahmedabad. You are at your cousin's wedding sitting at the men's table during dinner. Someone brings up that a well known female politician in your state who is unmarried and childless just won a big election. The men at the table are reacting to this news. You are giving your opinion on whether a woman who has no husband and no children can truly understand the needs of a family and whether she deserves the public's trust and respect.
